name: Saturn
fact: Saturn's density is 0.687 g/cm³ — less than water. Its ring system spans 282,000 km but is only ~10 metres thick.
bio: The least dense planet — it would float on water. The ring system spans 282,000 km but is only metres thick. The 26.73° tilt means Earth sees the rings at different angles across Saturn's 29-year orbit.
